compressed_view: Design system token selection and component reuse patterns. Token namespace priority: Status > Chart > Accent > Brand > Surface > Background > Text > Border. Never use Brand for feedback (use Status), never use raw Brand for data viz (use Chart). New tokens only when no semantic match exists AND raw literal would be required AND value is domain-specific. Token pipeline: tokens.json (source) via Style Dictionary to DesignTokens.swift, referenced by AppTheme.swift. CI gate: make tokens-check detects drift. Component promotion: extract to AppComponents.swift when pattern used in 2+ screens (AppMetricColumn, AppMetricTile, ProgressBar, AppLayout promoted). Decomposition threshold: v1 files >800 lines must decompose in v2. Analytics parameter formats: weight float kg, duration int seconds, enums snake_case. DS Evolution Queue tracked in feature-memory.md for gaps needing broader discussion.

task_signature.context: Token selection rationale and component reuse patterns. Used by /design and /ux when selecting tokens and by /analytics when choosing event parameter value formats. Reflects Home v2 and Training v2 DS evolution decisions.

learned_patterns.pattern: Token namespace selection — which AppColor.* enum for which use case
learned_patterns.decision: Use the most specific namespace. Priority: Status > Chart > Accent > Brand > Surface > Background > Text > Border. Never use Brand tokens for UI feedback (use Status). Never use raw Brand colors for data visualization (use Chart). Never use Surface tokens for typography (use Text).

namespace_guide.AppColor.Brand.*: Logo, primary action buttons, brand identity elements. Never for feedback states.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Background.*: Screen-level backgrounds (appPrimary, appSecondary, appTint, appWarmTint). Auth screens use authTop/authMiddle/authBottom (always dark).
namespace_guide.AppColor.Surface.*: Card backgrounds, sheet backgrounds, material overlays. Layering: primary < secondary < tertiary < elevated < materialLight < materialStrong.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Text.*: Typography only. primary=body, secondary=labels/captions, tertiary=metadata/timestamps (WCAG AA compliant). inverse* variants for dark surface text.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Border.*: Dividers and outlines only. strong=prominent, subtle=card edges, hairline=0.5pt pixel separators.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Accent.*: Domain-specific accents. recovery=recovery/readiness metric. sleep=sleep metrics. achievement=PRs/milestones. primary/secondary=interactive CTAs.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Status.*: System feedback ONLY. success=positive state, warning=caution, error=negative/destructive.
namespace_guide.AppColor.Chart.*: Data visualization ONLY. body=strength/body metrics, cardio=cardio metrics, sleep=sleep data, achievement=achievements, progress=goal progress, nutritionFat=nutrition fat macro, weight=weight tracking, hrv=HRV metric, heartRate=heart rate metric.

source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F8: chart tokens gap → T3 added weight/hrv/heartRate

learned_patterns.pattern: When to add new tokens vs reuse existing
learned_patterns.decision: Add new token when: (1) no existing token semantically covers the need, (2) a raw literal would otherwise be required, (3) the value is domain-specific enough to warrant a named token. Propose on feature branch. Token additions in Home v2: AppText.monoCaption (monospaced numeric display), AppSize.tabBarClearance (bottom padding for tab bar overlap). Training v2: AppSize.tabBarClearance (same need, confirmed reuse), AppText.monoCaption (confirmed reuse). Pending DS Evolution Queue tracked in feature-memory.md.
learned_patterns.new_token_process: T1-style task: add to AppTheme.swift → run make tokens-check → confirm pipeline passes → then use in view code.
source_executions: home-today-screen T1 (AppText/AppSize tokens), T2 (motion tokens), T3 (Chart tokens)
PR #78: 4 raw color literals fixed for 100% compliance

learned_patterns.pattern: Component promotion — AppComponents.swift additions from feature work
learned_patterns.decision: When a sub-view pattern is used in 2+ screens, promote to AppComponents.swift as a shared component. Home v2 promoted: AppMetricColumn (T4), AppMetricTile (T5). Training v2 promoted: 3 shared components. Promotion process: extract pattern → define in AppComponents.swift → replace in-feature usage → verify no duplicate definitions remain in view files.

promoted_components.AppMetricColumn: Vertical metric display with value + label. Added Home v2 T4. Use for dashboard-style metric columns.
promoted_components.AppMetricTile: Tappable metric tile with tint color + icon + value. Added Home v2 T5. Use for Home screen metric grid.
promoted_components.training_shared_components_3: 3 components promoted in Training v2 (exact names in feature-memory.md). Use for exercise-related displays.
promoted_components.ProgressBar: Reusable progress indicator. Added Nutrition v2. Used in Stats v2.
promoted_components.AppLayout: Layout constants enum (chartHeight, emptyStateMinHeight, chipMin/Ideal/Max, dotSize). Added Stats v2.

learned_patterns.pattern: Design token pipeline — tokens.json → Style Dictionary → DesignTokens.swift
learned_patterns.decision: Token pipeline: design-tokens/tokens.json is the source of truth → Style Dictionary transforms → DesignTokens.swift generated file → AppTheme.swift references DesignTokens.swift. The CI gate make tokens-check detects any drift (token referenced in AppTheme.swift but not in tokens.json, or vice versa). Adding a new token requires updating tokens.json first, then AppTheme.swift.
learned_patterns.gotcha: Never add a token to AppTheme.swift without a corresponding entry in design-tokens/tokens.json. tokens-check will fail in CI.
source_executions: CLAUDE.md Design System section: token pipeline description
v2-refactor-checklist B4: 'Run make tokens-check to verify nothing drifts the token pipeline'

learned_patterns.pattern: DS Evolution Queue — tracking pending tokens and components
learned_patterns.decision: When an audit identifies a token or component gap that can't be resolved in the current feature (e.g. it needs broader discussion), add it to the Pending DS Evolution Queue in docs/design-system/feature-memory.md. Home v2 added 8 new tokens and 2 component promotions to the queue (commit 844a2ba). Items in the queue are addressed in the earliest feature branch that needs them.
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json research.v2_substeps_completed: 'Pending DS Evolution Queue updated in feature-memory.md (8 new tokens, 2 component promotions queued)'

learned_patterns.pattern: Component architecture for extracted views (Training v2 decomposition)
learned_patterns.decision: Large monolithic view files (>500 lines) MUST be decomposed in the v2 refactor. Training v2 decomposed 2,135-line TrainingPlanView into: TrainingPlanView (533-line container) + StatusDropdown + MilestoneModal + ExerciseRow + SetCompletionIndicator + RestTimerView + SkeletonLoadingView. Each extracted view is a private struct within the v2/ directory unless it has cross-feature reuse potential (then promote to AppComponents.swift).
learned_patterns.decomposition_threshold: v1 files >800 lines should be decomposed in v2. Each sub-view gets its own file in the v2/ directory. Container stays at v2/OriginalName.swift.
source_executions: home-today-screen: v1 1029 lines → v2 703 lines (single file OK for simpler screen)

learned_patterns.pattern: Analytics parameter value formats — DS-level consistency
learned_patterns.decision: Consistent value formats across all events: weight always float kg (SI), duration always int seconds (SI), enums use snake_case (build_muscle, lose_fat, maintain, general_fitness). Muscle groups: push/pull/legs/cardio/rest. Meal types: breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack. Entry methods: manual/template/photo/barcode. These are de-facto design system constraints enforced through the taxonomy CSV.
source_executions: analytics-taxonomy.csv parameter columns for training_set_logged, meal_log, onboarding_goal_selected

anti_patterns.pattern: Creating ad-hoc components inside v2 view files instead of promoting to AppComponents.swift
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Private sub-views that are genuinely reusable across screens bloat individual view files and cause duplicate patterns. The test is: 'would 2+ screens benefit from this?' If yes, promote. If no, private struct in the v2/ file is fine.
anti_patterns.source_execution: v2-refactor-checklist B3: 'no new ad-hoc components inside the v2 file unless declared as private types'

anti_patterns.pattern: Using AppColor.Brand.* for system feedback states
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Brand primary is a vivid blue — using it for both CTAs and error states creates semantic confusion. Error states must use AppColor.Status.error, success states use AppColor.Status.success. Brand color is reserved for brand identity and interactive affordance.
anti_patterns.source_execution: AppTheme.swift enum structure separates Brand, Status, Accent into distinct namespaces precisely for this reason

anti_patterns.pattern: Adding tokens directly to AppTheme.swift without updating tokens.json
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: make tokens-check fails in CI because tokens.json is out of sync. Always update design-tokens/tokens.json first, then add the semantic alias to AppTheme.swift.
anti_patterns.source_execution: CLAUDE.md Design System token pipeline; v2-refactor-checklist B4

speedup_instructions: When /design or /ux needs to select a token: check namespace_guide first to identify the correct AppColor.* enum, then verify the token exists in FitTracker/Services/AppTheme.swift. When a new token is needed: follow the new_token_process (tokens.json → AppTheme.swift → make tokens-check). When a component may be promotable: check promoted_components list first — AppMetricColumn and AppMetricTile already exist and cover metric display patterns.
